title: HTTP API Resources, Representations, Errors, and Handler Layers · Runtime Contract: Feed Go Resource Model
summary: Routes mirror tables, handlers parse, query, and render directly, status codes are ad hoc, response shapes drift, and request cancellation never reaches storage. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go resource model increment in consistent feed-reader HTTP API. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case pract…
objectives: Design tenant, source, subscription, entry, collection, search, and reader-state resources around client decisions rather than tables.
Stop claims, cancel fetches, bound drain time, join workers, finalize or abandon leases safely, close pools, and report incomplete work.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Resource Model · Theory Feed Go Resource Model
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 2208 during a publisher-fairness incident rehearsal. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource semantics, layered handlers, problem details, cancellation, and response finality; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then interleave one schedule, lease, fencing, goroutine, cancellation, or shutdown path and prove ownership. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Feed Go Resource Model contract for the supplied data.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using design and tenant plus specific case evidence.
